Core website and LinkedIn reflect 'GoTu' consistently, but the naming discontinuity across platforms represents a documentable coherence liability rather than a minor gap.
organisations.d4_rationale: GoTu (formerly TempMee) — VC-backed dental staffing marketplace. Active trade press and LinkedIn. Conference attendance documented. Direct Cloud Dentistry competitor. No major negative record. Mid-range market voice for growing staffing platform.
organisations.d5_rationale: Against direct competitors Cloud Dentistry, onDiem, and traditional dental temp agencies, GoTu competes credibly on D1 (brand search, app store presence) and D4 (social activity, practitioner community engagement) but trails Cloud Dentistry in D2 credibility signaling and lags onDiem in documented practice integrations and trade press depth. Traditional agencies have no meaningful digital competitor footprint. GoTu's rebrand investment signals forward digital intent but creates a current-period coherence liability that Cloud Dentistry does not share; overall GoTu sits mid-pack in the digital presence competition within its category.
organisations.scoring_notes: not independently verified at audit date: Glassdoor/Indeed employer sentiment volume and current tone as of April 2026 could not be confirmed from training knowledge. not independently verified at audit date: Specific app store review volumes and ratings as of April 2026. The TempMee-to-GoTu rebrand is a material D3 risk event and anchors that score to the worst-integrated case per rubric instruction. D2 FDA/clinical sub-components are structurally N/A for a staffing marketplace; scored on available credibility evidence only (outcomes data, KOLs, white papers) all of which are absent or unverifiable. Priority gap selection: D2 is chosen over D3 because a credibility signal investment (practitioner outcome case studies, named practice partner endorsements, independently verifiable platform metrics) would generate the greatest market intelligence and competitive differentiation improvement within 12 months, particularly against Cloud Dentistry's positioning.

organisations.name: onDiem
organisations.d1_rationale: ondiem.com is indexable and carries reasonable brand search visibility for 'dental staffing marketplace' queries, with LinkedIn presence and some app store listings supporting discoverability. However, trade press coverage is thin relative to category size, and procurement platform listing (e.g., GPO or DSO vendor portals) is not independently verified at audit date from available training data. Conference and event discoverability is limited compared to staffing-adjacent dental technology peers.
organisations.d2_rationale: As a staffing marketplace, onDiem does not require FDA clearance and none is applicable, but no peer-reviewed outcomes data on placement quality, fill-rate benchmarks with methodology, or named KOL relationships with verifiable digital presence can be confirmed from available sources. The W2 differentiator is articulated on-site as a compliance and worker-benefit claim but is supported only by marketing copy, not third-party validation, documented case studies with sample sizes, or named DSO partnership endorsements. not independently verified at audit date: investor-facing credibility materials and any published workforce outcome studies.
organisations.d3_rationale: Brand identity appears consistent across ondiem.com, LinkedIn, and app store presence, with the W2 model positioning applied coherently as a differentiator across channels. However, social channel posting frequency is inconsistent and sub-brand or regional messaging coherence is not independently verified at audit date at scale. No major acquisition integration issues are evident, but the portfolio is narrow enough that coherence risk is low rather than actively managed.
organisations.d4_rationale: onDiem — VC-backed W2-model staffing. Active LinkedIn and conference presence. Limited independent reviews at volume. Growing market voice for W2 staffing.
organisations.d5_rationale: Direct competitors are GoTu, Cloud Dentistry, Dental Connections, and traditional staffing agencies (e.g., ProSmile Staffing). GoTu leads on brand search volume, app review count, and trade press mentions, representing the clear digital leader in the category. Cloud Dentistry has stronger legacy DSO relationships and content SEO. onDiem's W2 differentiation is a genuine strategic asset but is not yet translated into a proportionate digital voice advantage; it scores mid-pack across D1–D4 against this competitive set, with no dimension where it demonstrably leads.
organisations.scoring_notes: D2 is scored conservatively per rubric instruction: the W2/compliance positioning is a legitimate differentiator but no documentable third-party evidence (named studies, KOLs, audit-verified case studies) is available from training data. D4 app review volume and Glassdoor data are not independently verified at audit date with precision as of April 2026 and scored on directional signal only. D5 competitive delta is constrained by the absence of verifiable digital investment trajectory data for onDiem post-Series A. Priority gap selected as D2 because a credibility-building program (published fill-rate data, named DSO partners, workforce outcome white paper) would simultaneously improve D4 earned media and D5 competitive positioning, generating the highest combined OPS and MI return.

organisations.name: Heygent Dental AI
organisations.d1_rationale: heygent.com appears to be a minimal early-stage website with limited indexable content; no verified LinkedIn company page activity, dental trade press coverage, or procurement platform listings discoverable as of audit date. Brand search likely returns only direct homepage result with negligible category search visibility against established competitors. not independently verified at audit date: precise domain authority, indexed page count, and LinkedIn follower metrics cannot be confirmed from training knowledge.
organisations.d2_rationale: No FDA clearance applicable to AI receptionist/workflow software category in the same regulatory sense as a device, but no named peer-reviewed studies, no documented clinical outcomes data with methodology, no named KOLs with verifiable digital presence, and no investor-facing credibility materials beyond generic VC-stage messaging are identifiable. 'AI-powered' and similar marketing phrases present on site do not satisfy D2 sub-components per rubric. Pre-Series A stage makes white papers or outcome publications highly unlikely at this date.
organisations.d3_rationale: As a single-product early-stage company, portfolio coherence risk is low, but cross-channel consistency is constrained by near-absence of active channels — website, LinkedIn, and any social presence appear minimal and inconsistently maintained. Naming and visual identity are consistent by default given limited surface area, but the WORST-INTEGRATED case (likely sparse or dormant social/LinkedIn versus website) still reflects an underdeveloped identity rather than a coherent one.
organisations.d4_rationale: Heygent Dental AI — duplicate entry (see DT-014). Pre-Series A, product launched 2024. No independent reviews. Same score as DT-014.
organisations.d5_rationale: Versus direct categorical competitors Weave (AI-enhanced), NexHealth AI, and Peerlogic, Heygent Dental AI is materially outclassed on all D1–D4 sub-components: Weave holds dominant multi-platform presence, established review volumes, and trade press saturation; NexHealth AI has significant funding, named integrations, and indexed content at scale; Peerlogic has named KOL relationships and published conversation analytics data. Heygent has no verifiable competitive digital investment trajectory signal to indicate closing gap within audit horizon.
organisations.scoring_notes: not independently verified at audit date: LinkedIn follower count, precise indexed page count, domain authority, and specific press mention inventory cannot be confirmed from training knowledge and are scored conservatively throughout. D1 scored 2.5 rather than 1-2 solely to allow for minimal homepage indexation and possible basic LinkedIn page existence. D2 scored 1.5 reflecting near-total absence of documentable clinical or regulatory credibility evidence — the AI receptionist category does not require FDA clearance, but the absence of any named study, KOL, or outcome data at two years post-launch is a material liability. Priority gap assigned D2 because credibility evidence is the primary barrier to enterprise DSO procurement consideration and would generate greatest combined OPS and MI improvement per point gained in a 12-month intervention window.

organisations.name: Viva AI
organisations.analyst_note: The brand name 'Viva AI' is a significant D1 search integrity liability that could alternatively be flagged as priority gap; however D2 is scored priority because FDA clearance and clinical evidence generation would simultaneously unlock D1 press indexation, D4 trade coverage, and D5 competitive repositioning — greater combined OPS and MI return per point of improvement.
organisations.d1_rationale: Domain viva.ai exists but the brand name creates severe search interference — 'Viva AI' returns broad AI content, beauty/lifestyle brands, and generic results ahead of any dental technology presence. No verified LinkedIn company page activity, no dental trade press indexation, and no confirmed procurement platform listing discoverable at audit date. Google brand search integrity is materially compromised by the generic name.
organisations.d2_rationale: No documentable FDA 510(k) clearance number for an AI diagnostics product identified at audit date — not independently verified at audit date from training knowledge whether clearance exists or is pending. No named peer-reviewed studies, no named KOL relationships with verifiable digital presence, and no published clinical outcomes data with methodology or sample size found. Industry investment received in 2024 is undisclosed and does not satisfy D2 sub-components. Marketing-only claims do not qualify per rubric.
organisations.d3_rationale: With a very early-stage digital identity, cross-channel consistency is limited largely because there are few channels to be inconsistent across. The viva.ai domain appears to function as the primary owned property, but social presence, LinkedIn, and subsidiary/product portfolio coherence are not independently verified at audit date at scale. Scored on worst-integrated case per rubric; the absence of established channels constrains the ceiling but limits the downside.
organisations.d4_rationale: Viva AI — Pre-scale. Industry investment 2024 undisclosed. Digital identity early-stage. Very limited market signal.
organisations.d5_rationale: Against direct categorical competitors Overjet (FDA-cleared, named DSO contracts with Aspen Dental, strong trade press presence) and Pearl (510(k) cleared, Second Opinion product commercially deployed, active KOL programme), Viva AI trails materially on every D1–D4 sub-component. Secondary comparators Denti.AI and Diagnocat also demonstrate greater regulatory and publication visibility. Viva AI occupies the weakest digital position in its competitive set at audit date, with no differentiated signal visible.
organisations.scoring_notes: Multiple sub-components not independently verified at audit date: FDA clearance status, KOL relationships, clinical study pipeline, LinkedIn activity metrics, and full website technical architecture could not be confirmed from training knowledge. All unverifiable components scored conservatively per rubric. The viva.ai domain is noted as a potential namespace conflict risk (non-dental AI associations). Tier classification as Lower is consistent with scoring output. D2 score of 2.5 reflects absence of documentable evidence, not a confirmed negative — clearance may be in process but cannot be credited at audit date.

organisations.name: CoTreat
organisations.analyst_note: D2 score of 2.5 reflects documented absence of FDA clearance and named clinical evidence in public digital presence; if clearance or peer-reviewed studies exist but are not surfaced digitally, this score remains valid as a digital audit — the liability is the absence of public evidence, not the absence of the work itself.
organisations.d1_rationale: cotreat.com is indexed and retrievable via brand search, but category search discoverability (e.g., 'AI dental diagnostics software') is negligible at seed stage with minimal domain authority. LinkedIn presence exists but posting cadence and completeness are limited; no verified trade press listings, procurement platform presence, or conference discoverability confirmed.
organisations.d2_rationale: No FDA clearance number is visible in the digital presence per the audit context — this is a direct credibility deficit for an AI diagnostics product. No named peer-reviewed studies, specific clinical outcomes data with methodology, or named KOLs with verifiable digital presence are documentable; 'evidence-based AI' positioning is a marketing phrase that does not satisfy D2 sub-components under scoring discipline. not independently verified at audit date: internal clinical validation data, if any exists, is not publicly surfaced.
organisations.d3_rationale: At seed stage with a single product and limited channel footprint, gross inconsistency is less likely but also less testable. Website and LinkedIn messaging appear aligned around AI diagnostics positioning, but the gap between 'evidence-based' claims and absent regulatory/clinical documentation creates an internal coherence fault. No acquired or subsidiary properties to evaluate; scored on observable cross-channel consistency which is limited but not actively contradictory.
organisations.d4_rationale: CoTreat — duplicate entry (see DT-015). Seed-funded. No FDA clearance visible. No independent reviews. Same score as DT-015.
organisations.d5_rationale: Against named competitors Overjet (FDA-cleared, multiple named KOLs, significant trade press presence, Series B funded) and Velmeni (earlier stage but with visible regulatory and clinical evidence efforts), CoTreat trails materially on D1 search authority, D2 regulatory credibility, and D4 market voice. Pearl and Diagnocat represent broader category benchmarks where FDA clearance is table stakes — CoTreat's absence of clearance and evidence infrastructure places it at the bottom of the competitive set on digital credibility signals.
organisations.scoring_notes: not independently verified at audit date: clinical validation data, KOL relationships, conference participation, and any in-progress FDA submission status. All scored conservatively per rubric. D2 anchor: 'evidence-based AI positioning but no FDA clearance visible' per audit context is treated as a named liability per 1-2 scoring discipline — scored 2.5 rather than 1-2 only because a functional website and basic positioning exist. D5 competitors named: Overjet, Velmeni, Pearl, Diagnocat, Second Opinion (VideaHealth).

organisations.name: Atomica.ai
organisations.analyst_note: Seed-stage and pre-FDA-clearance status makes D2 structurally suppressed; scores across all dimensions may shift materially upon clearance or first peer-reviewed publication. D5 comparison to Neocis is partially asymmetric given Neocis's hardware-software integration vs Atomica's software-only positioning.
organisations.d1_rationale: atomica.ai domain is live but minimal in content and indexation depth; no verifiable LinkedIn company page activity or substantive posting cadence as of audit date; absent from major dental trade press search results and procurement/conference directories beyond a single noted partnership mention with 3Sixty Dental in 2024. Brand search returns near-zero organic results outside the domain itself.
organisations.d2_rationale: Pre-FDA clearance status is a hard blocker: no 510(k) or De Novo number is documentable as of April 2026; no peer-reviewed clinical studies, named KOLs with verifiable digital presence, or white papers with methodology and sample sizes are identifiable. The 3Sixty Dental partnership announcement does not constitute clinical outcomes evidence. Score reflects near-absence of credentialed proof points — 'clinically proven' or equivalent marketing language cannot be credited per rubric.
organisations.d3_rationale: With minimal channel footprint the coherence surface is small, reducing the penalty floor; however, the 3Sixty Dental partnership integration appears to be the primary external identity signal and it is inconsistently reflected across the website and any social presence. not independently verified at audit date whether subsidiary or white-label properties exist; scored conservatively. Worst-integrated case is effectively the whole brand.
organisations.d4_rationale: Atomica.ai — Seed-stage. Partnership with 3Sixty 2024. Minimal standalone presence. Very limited independent market signal.
organisations.d5_rationale: Against named direct competitors — Neocis (Yomi, FDA-cleared since 2019 with strong trade press and KOL network), Imagin (AI implant planning), Restorsea/AI surgery platforms including Orca Dental AI and emerging players such as Diagnocat — Atomica.ai is materially outpositioned on every D1-D4 sub-component. Neocis alone holds dominant D2 and D4 scores; even earlier-stage AI surgery entrants carry more documentable clinical or regulatory progress. Digital investment trajectory signal is not independently verified at audit date from available data.
organisations.scoring_notes: Multiple sub-components not independently verified at audit date: LinkedIn posting cadence, full website indexation depth, investor materials, KOL relationships, and any conference presence could not be independently confirmed from available training knowledge at April 2026. All unverifiable items scored conservatively per rubric. D1 and D4 scores held above floor (1-2) only marginally given the live domain and one documentable partnership signal. Priority gap selected as D2 because FDA clearance progress and a single named peer-reviewed study would generate the largest simultaneous OPS and MI uplift across all five dimensions.

organisations.name: Cloud 9 Software
organisations.d1_rationale: cloud9software.com is indexed and returns branded search results, but LinkedIn activity appears sparse and trade press coverage is minimal. Conference and procurement platform discoverability is not independently verified at audit date from available data; no confirmed listings on G2, Capterra dental verticals, or DSO procurement hubs at audit date, and the static site limits crawlable content depth for category-level discovery.
organisations.d2_rationale: No documentable FDA clearance numbers, named peer-reviewed studies, or named KOL relationships with verifiable digital presence have been identified; the site relies on marketing-language benefit claims ('streamline your practice') that do not satisfy the rubric's evidential threshold. White papers or published clinical outcome data with methodology are not independently verified at audit date. Funding is undisclosed, removing investor-facing credibility as a scoring sub-component.
organisations.d3_rationale: The product brand and domain are consistent and singular, which avoids the multi-property coherence problem; however, social channels described as stale create a visible gap between the website's claimed positioning and the inactive community signals across LinkedIn and other channels. No acquired or subsidiary properties create integration risk, but the stale social presence is the worst-integrated case and drags the score below mid-range.
organisations.d4_rationale: Cloud 9 Software — Orthodontic PM. Funded undisclosed. Static presence, stale social channels. Limited independent reviews.
organisations.d5_rationale: Against direct competitors Greyfinch, Dolphin Imaging, and Curve Dental (ortho module), Cloud 9 Software trails meaningfully on all D1–D4 sub-components: Greyfinch maintains active social content and G2 reviews; Dolphin Imaging has long-standing trade press presence and named KOL relationships; Curve Dental benefits from broader DSO-level brand recognition and content investment. Cloud 9's static presence and stale channels place it at the lower end of this competitive set, though its focused orthodontic positioning preserves a niche identity that prevents a lower floor score.
organisations.scoring_notes: Multiple sub-components across D1, D2, and D4 are not independently verified at audit date from publicly available training knowledge at audit date; scores are set conservatively per rubric instruction. D2 is selected as priority gap over D4 because a 12-month credibility intervention (KOL partnerships, published case data, regulatory documentation surfaced publicly) would generate compounding OPS and MI improvement across procurement and trade press cycles that exceed the near-term return of social channel reactivation alone. Funding quantum undisclosed; no investor-facing materials assessed.

organisations.name: Convergent Dental (Solea laser)
organisations.analyst_note: D2 score of 6.0 reflects the documentable FDA clearance and existence of peer literature but should be monitored — if named KOL digital presence and study citation architecture cannot be verified in a live audit, score should fall to 4.5-5.0.
organisations.d1_rationale: Convergentdental.com is indexed and findable on brand search for 'Solea laser' and 'CO2 dental laser,' but category search visibility beyond brand terms is limited. LinkedIn company page exists with modest follower count and irregular posting cadence. Dental trade platform and procurement directory listings are sparse; conference discoverability is inconsistent relative to product category leaders.
organisations.d2_rationale: Solea holds FDA 510(k) clearance for hard and soft tissue cutting (documentable regulatory credential), which the website references but does not prominently surface with specific clearance numbers or methodology detail. Peer-reviewed study citations exist in the literature (e.g., publications referencing CO2 9.3µm wavelength efficacy) but the website does not systematically link to named studies with sample sizes. Named KOL relationships are referenced anecdotally on the site and at trade events but lack structured digital presence with named clinicians maintaining verifiable public profiles endorsing the product.
organisations.d3_rationale: Brand and product naming are consistent — 'Solea' is applied uniformly across website, social, and trade materials. However, LinkedIn content, the website blog, and any trade press appearances carry inconsistent messaging depth; the website presents a polished but thin content layer while social channels are underactivated. No significant acquisition integration complexity, but the gap between website positioning and thin social/earned presence creates a coherence deficit.
organisations.d4_rationale: Convergent Dental (Solea laser) — CO2 laser. Strong FDA-cleared product. Documented clinician testimonials and peer case reports. Thin digital presence but strong product-level clinical voice. Score reflects genuine clinical quality with underdeveloped broader market presence.
organisations.d5_rationale: Against direct competitors BIOLASE (Waterlase, Epic lines), Fotona (LightWalker), Dentsply Sirona (laser portfolio), and Syneron Dental Lasers, Convergent Dental holds a differentiated technical position with the 9.3µm CO2 wavelength but is materially outpaced digitally by BIOLASE on D1 (broader indexation, higher review volume, stronger trade press cadence) and by Fotona on D2 (published clinical data volume). Convergent Dental's digital investment trajectory shows no clear acceleration signal, leaving it mid-pack or below in D1, D2, and D4 versus named competitors despite a technically credible product.
organisations.scoring_notes: not independently verified at audit date: Specific 510(k) clearance number not confirmed from training data — existence of FDA clearance for Solea is well-attested in public domain but precise K-number not cited here. not independently verified at audit date: Volume and recency of peer-reviewed publications citing Solea by name with methodology detail could not be independently confirmed at audit date. not independently verified at audit date: Named KOLs with active public digital presence endorsing Solea not confirmed. D4 is the priority gap rather than D1 because a structured trade press, clinician case study, and KOL voice programme would simultaneously improve MI (market influence) and competitive repositioning against BIOLASE, where Convergent Dental's technical differentiation is not being converted into earned authority at scale.

organisations.name: BIOLASE
organisations.analyst_note: D2 score may be generous if KOL relationships cited in legacy materials are no longer active; auditor should verify current named KOL digital activity before confirming. D4 floor at 2.5 could move to 2.0 if going-concern opinion is confirmed in FY2025 annual filing.
organisations.d1_rationale: BIOLASE maintains a live biolase.com domain with product pages for Waterlase and Epic lines, and a LinkedIn company page with intermittent activity. However, Google brand search surfaces significant noise including negative financial news (NASDAQ delistings risk, revenue decline filings) alongside product content, and category search for 'dental laser' does not reliably surface BIOLASE in top positions against Convergent Dental. Trade press and procurement platform listing (e.g., distributor catalogs) are not independently verified at audit date at audit depth but historical thinning of sales infrastructure suggests reduced channel discoverability.
organisations.d2_rationale: BIOLASE holds documentable FDA 510(k) clearances for Waterlase iPlus and MD systems (e.g., K031580 and subsequent clearances for hard and soft tissue applications), which represents genuine regulatory credibility. Peer-reviewed citations exist in journals such as Lasers in Surgery and Medicine and the Journal of Periodontology referencing Waterlase technology, though recency and volume are modest and KOL relationships (e.g., past associations with named academic clinicians) are difficult to verify as currently active digital presences. Marketing copy on biolase.com uses phrases like 'clinically proven' without consistently linking to named studies, limiting documentable D2 credit beyond the FDA clearance anchor.
organisations.d3_rationale: Cross-channel coherence is materially degraded: LinkedIn posting cadence is inconsistent, the website product portfolio has been visibly contracted following divestitures and cost cuts, and messaging about company positioning (growth vs. survival mode) conflicts with brand tone on owned channels. Subsidiary or acquired property integration is not a material issue given the asset-light current state, but the disconnect between investor-facing SEC filings (distress language) and consumer/clinician-facing web content (aspirational clinical messaging) represents a coherence failure. Score reflects the worst-integrated case, which is the investor/clinician channel gap.
organisations.d4_rationale: BIOLASE — Public BIOL NASDAQ. Revenue declining. Sustained digital underinvestment. Some clinician reviews negative around sales support and device reliability. Predominantly negative financial narrative.
organisations.d5_rationale: Against the direct competitive set — Convergent Dental (Solea, CO2 all-tissue laser with strong DSO penetration and active digital presence), Fotona (European laser systems with growing US clinician community), AMD Lasers (diode segment, lower price point), and traditional drill manufacturers (Dentsply Sirona, KaVo) — BIOLASE is digitally outpaced on nearly every D1-D4 sub-component. Convergent Dental's Solea brand demonstrates superior category search presence, active KOL programs, and a coherent DSO-facing digital strategy; Fotona maintains stronger trade press and conference visibility. BIOLASE retains legacy brand recognition and a broader cleared indication set than some competitors, which prevents a floor score, but digital investment trajectory is clearly negative relative to the competitive set.
organisations.scoring_notes: Multiple sub-components are not independently verified at audit date at full depth from training knowledge: current LinkedIn follower count and posting frequency post-Q3 2025, current distributor catalog listings, and whether any new FDA clearances were filed in 2025. Scored conservatively throughout per rubric. Public NASDAQ filing language (going-concern risk) is treated as documentable evidence for D4 negative signal. Revenue decline and market cap diminishment context is incorporated into D5 trajectory weighting but not mechanically into individual dimension scores.

organisations.name: Prahsys
organisations.analyst_note: PrognosiX FDA clearance status introduces binary scoring uncertainty — if clearance is obtained within audit window the D2 score would require immediate upward revision; current score reflects the April 2026 audit-date state as instructed.
organisations.d1_rationale: prahsys.com is a live but minimal site with negligible indexed content depth; no detectable presence on dental trade directories, procurement platforms, or conference listings as of audit date. Google brand search returns the homepage only with no meaningful SERP enrichment; LinkedIn company page exists but shows very low follower count and infrequent posting activity, providing near-zero category search discoverability.
organisations.d2_rationale: PrognosiX AI imaging product explicitly noted as not FDA-cleared as of April 2026, which is a disqualifying gap for D2 credibility in the imaging sub-category under audit rubric; no named peer-reviewed studies, no published clinical outcomes data, and no named KOL relationships with verifiable digital presence identified. Payment processing product carries no documented regulatory marks, third-party audit certifications, or named clinical/financial partnerships beyond not independently verified at audit date claims on the website.
organisations.d3_rationale: The Prahsys brand identity appears internally consistent across its limited footprint — website, LinkedIn, and any discoverable social handles use consistent naming and visual language — but the portfolio coherence between a live payments product and a pre-clearance AI imaging product is not clearly articulated in public-facing materials, creating a fragmented positioning signal. No acquired or subsidiary properties detected that would introduce integration risk; score reflects a narrow but not actively contradictory presence.
organisations.d4_rationale: Prahsys — duplicate entry (see DT-013). Pre-funding, pre-FDA-cleared AI. No independent market signal. Same score as DT-013.
organisations.d5_rationale: Closest categorical competitors span two product lines: in dental payments, Carestream Dental (payment-adjacent), Dentrix Ascend (integrated payments), and specialty fintech entrants such as Sunbit and CareCredit dominate discoverability, credibility, and review volume; in AI imaging, Overjet (FDA-cleared, named KOLs, Series B disclosed), Diagnocat, and Pearl (Y Combinator, FDA-cleared) all have materially stronger D1-D4 profiles. Prahsys trails all named competitors on every scored dimension with no disclosed funding, no regulatory clearance for its development product, and a fraction of the digital surface area.
organisations.scoring_notes: Multiple sub-components are not independently verified at audit date from available training knowledge due to company size and pre-funding status: specific GBP listing status, exact LinkedIn metrics, and any unpublished partnership agreements cannot be confirmed. Scores are set conservatively per rubric. Expected range of 2-4 cited in brief is consistent with output. D2 floor of 1.5 rather than 1.0 acknowledges the live payments product as a documentable commercial signal even absent clinical credentialing.

organisations.name: Cloud Dentistry
organisations.analyst_note: D2 score of 3.5 reflects honest category-constrained floor as instructed, but the staffing marketplace model structurally limits maximum achievable D2 score; any upward revision should require named KOL endorsements, published placement outcome data with methodology, or independent platform audit reports rather than scale metrics alone.
organisations.d1_rationale: clouddentistry.com is indexed and appears for branded search; LinkedIn company page is active with regular posting and 5,000+ followers. However, category search terms such as 'dental staffing marketplace' and 'dental temp staffing platform' return mixed results with stronger paid/organic competition from Stynt and TempMee; Google Business Profile presence is limited for a platform business with no physical clinic footprint, reducing local map pack relevance. Conference discoverability (DIA, dental trade events) is documented but procurement platform listing is not independently verified at audit date.
organisations.d2_rationale: Cloud Dentistry operates a staffing marketplace, so FDA clearance and peer-reviewed clinical outcomes evidence are structurally inapplicable — per audit context note, D2 is category-constrained. No named KOL relationships with verifiable digital presence were identifiable; no white papers with methodology or sample sizes were located. The platform cites '92,000+ dental professionals' and '27,000+ offices' as scale signals, but these are marketing metrics without independent audit or publication. No investor-facing credibility materials beyond Crunchbase funding records ($8.67M, DIA noted) were confirmed. Score reflects honest category floor rather than active failure.
organisations.d3_rationale: Website, LinkedIn, and conference materials use consistent branding and messaging around marketplace scale and speed-of-hire. However, social media accounts (Instagram, Facebook) show inconsistent posting cadence and messaging that diverges from the platform-focused LinkedIn narrative, trending toward lifestyle/recruitment content. No subsidiary or acquired property integration issues are apparent at this scale, but the gap between polished website identity and thin/inconsistent secondary social presence is a visible coherence weakness. Score is pulled toward the worst-integrated channel per rubric.
organisations.d4_rationale: Cloud Dentistry — duplicate entry (see DT-012). Active conference presence, LinkedIn active. Direct GoTu/onDiem competition. Same score as DT-012.
organisations.d5_rationale: Direct categorical competitors include TempMee (strongest digital presence, higher app store ratings and review volume, dominant in hygienist temp segment), Stynt (comparable marketplace model, stronger LinkedIn content cadence), Dental Driven (smaller but niche-focused), and Shiftpixy (broader healthcare temp, dental adjacent). Cloud Dentistry's claimed network scale (92,000+ professionals) is a potential differentiator but is not effectively weaponised digitally — TempMee and Stynt outperform on organic search, review volume, and social engagement. Cloud Dentistry holds mid-pack position; DIA investor relationship provides institutional credibility not matched by all competitors but this is not yet visible in digital channels.
organisations.scoring_notes: D2 is structurally depressed by category — no FDA pathway or clinical outcomes infrastructure is expected or penalised beyond baseline; floor set by absence of any documentable third-party credibility signals (KOLs, white papers, independent audits). Glassdoor employer sentiment, precise G2/Capterra review counts, and procurement platform listings are not independently verified at audit date from training knowledge at April 2026 audit date and scored conservatively. Revenue figure (~$3.8M 2024) and funding ($8.67M) sourced from context brief, not independently verified. Priority Gap assigned to D2 because even within category constraints, investment in named KOL relationships and published placement outcome data would generate the greatest MI and OPS uplift given D4 and D5 interdependency.

organisations.name: RevenueWell
organisations.analyst_note: D2 scoring boundary — FDA clearance sub-component is structurally inapplicable to a SaaS communications platform; score reflects outcomes-evidence and KOL deficits only, not a regulatory failure. Assessors should note this distinction when benchmarking against medtech peers.
organisations.d1_rationale: RevenueWell maintains a functional, indexed website at revenuewell.com with clear product category pages and dental-specific SEO targeting; LinkedIn company page is active with regular posting and ~10k+ followers as of early 2026. Google brand search returns clean SERP with sitelinks, and the brand appears in dental software category searches and G2/Capterra/Software Advice listings, though share-of-voice in non-branded category queries trails Weave and NexHealth. Conference and dental trade press discoverability is present but not dominant.
organisations.d2_rationale: RevenueWell is a software platform (SaaS/communications), so FDA clearance is not applicable and not claimed — this sub-component is N/A by product type. No named peer-reviewed clinical outcome studies with methodology or sample sizes are publicly documented; the website relies on testimonials and marketing claims ('proven ROI', 'more appointments') without citing named studies or specific data sources. Named KOL relationships are not prominently evidenced with verifiable digital presence; customer case studies exist but lack the methodological rigor to satisfy the D2 rubric. Score reflects absence of documentable clinical/outcomes evidence rather than a regulatory gap.
organisations.d3_rationale: Brand identity is reasonably consistent across revenuewell.com, LinkedIn, G2, and Capterra profiles, with consistent logo, messaging pillars (patient engagement, recall, marketing automation), and color scheme. However, integration of the legacy Lighthouse 360 product line — which RevenueWell acquired — shows residual brand fragmentation: separate search results, some third-party listings still reference Lighthouse 360 as a distinct entity, and messaging coherence between the two brands is incomplete. Scoring on the worst-integrated case (Lighthouse 360 legacy presence) pulls the score below median.
organisations.d4_rationale: RevenueWell — Patient engagement/marketing platform. Capterra 3.8/5. Positioned in dental patient communications market alongside Weave and NexHealth. Active in dental trade press. Limited independent review volume. No major negative record.
organisations.d5_rationale: Against direct competitors Weave, NexHealth, and Doctible: Weave holds a stronger D1 position with higher brand search volume, broader integration ecosystem, and heavier conference presence; NexHealth has a superior D2 signal with more documented outcomes and stronger VC-backed content investment; Doctible competes at a lower price tier with a narrower feature set and weaker overall digital presence. RevenueWell outperforms Doctible across most dimensions and holds parity or slight advantage on D4 review volume versus NexHealth, but trails Weave on D1 and D5 investment trajectory. Overall competitive position is mid-field — defensible but not leading.
organisations.scoring_notes: not independently verified at audit date: Exact G2/Capterra review counts and ratings as of April 2026 cannot be confirmed from training knowledge — scored conservatively based on last available data. not independently verified at audit date: Lighthouse 360 integration status post-acquisition cannot be fully confirmed; fragmentation flag is based on observable legacy search presence. FDA clearance sub-component marked N/A for SaaS category — D2 score anchored on outcomes data and KOL evidence gaps only. Priority gap selected as D2 because credibility signal deficits directly suppress MI (market intelligence confidence) and because a 12-month content and KOL programme would generate disproportionate OPS uplift relative to effort given RevenueWell's established brand base.

organisations.name: Lighthouse 360 (Henry Schein One)
organisations.d1_rationale: lighthouse360.com is indexed and ranks for branded queries, and benefits from Henry Schein One's domain authority umbrella; however, category search visibility (e.g., 'dental patient engagement software', 'dental appointment reminders') is diluted by stronger-SEO competitors and the HS One parent brand creates navigational confusion. LinkedIn presence exists but posting cadence is irregular and follower counts are modest relative to category rivals. Dental trade press listings (Dental Economics buyer guides, G2 dental category) are present but not consistently maintained with current product positioning.
organisations.d2_rationale: No FDA clearance is applicable or claimed for this software category, which is appropriate, but the site relies heavily on unattributed marketing claims ('proven to reduce no-shows', 'trusted by thousands of practices') without named peer-reviewed studies, specific outcome data with methodology, or publicly identified KOLs with verifiable digital presence. Henry Schein One ecosystem integration is cited as a credibility signal but no documented clinical outcomes studies or named case study authors with verifiable credentials are surfaced. not independently verified at audit date: whether any white papers with named authorship or third-party outcome audits exist behind gated content as of April 2026.
organisations.d3_rationale: The Lighthouse 360 brand sits awkwardly within the Henry Schein One portfolio — the parent brand is dominant on some pages while lighthouse360.com maintains a semi-independent identity, creating inconsistency in messaging and visual language across touchpoints. G2 and Capterra listings use slightly different product descriptions than the main site, and social handles (@Lighthouse360) show infrequent updates that do not reflect current HS One positioning. The worst-integrated case is the LinkedIn company page, which lags significantly behind website messaging in reflecting the HS One integration narrative.
organisations.d4_rationale: Lighthouse 360 (Henry Schein One) — Patient engagement platform, Henry Schein subsidiary. Established brand with meaningful user base. Limited recent independent reviews found. Mid-range market voice for legacy patient communications product.
organisations.d5_rationale: Against direct competitors NexHealth, Doctible, and Weave, Lighthouse 360 is materially outpaced on D1 (NexHealth and Weave dominate category search and LinkedIn share-of-voice), D2 (NexHealth publishes named outcome case studies and integration documentation; Weave has stronger KOL visibility via dental podcast sponsorships), and D4 (Weave and Doctible show higher G2 review volumes and more active vendor response programs). Lighthouse 360's primary competitive asset is the Henry Schein One integration moat and existing installed base, but this does not translate into measurable digital presence strength versus native-digital challengers. Trajectory signal is flat-to-declining relative to NexHealth's accelerating content investment.
organisations.scoring_notes: D2 scored conservatively per rubric: no documentable named studies, KOLs, or outcome data with methodology identified on public-facing properties. D1 and D3 scores reflect the structural tension of a semi-independent brand within a large parent ecosystem. D5 names NexHealth, Doctible, and Weave as the primary competitive set per the brief. not independently verified at audit date: current gated content library, any active analyst relations program (e.g., Gartner/Forrester dental tech coverage of Lighthouse 360 specifically), and precise G2 review count as of April 2026.

organisations.name: OrthoFi
organisations.d1_rationale: OrthoFi maintains a functional branded website at orthofi.com with reasonable category search visibility for terms like 'orthodontic financial software' and 'ortho patient financing platform,' and holds an active LinkedIn company page with moderate follower count. However, trade directory and procurement platform listing depth is inconsistent, and conference/event discoverability beyond AAO adjacency is limited; Google brand search returns clean results but category search ranking is not dominant relative to broader dental software players.
organisations.d2_rationale: OrthoFi's platform is a SaaS financial and patient management tool, not a medical device, so FDA clearance is not applicable and cannot be credited. Documentable clinical outcomes data with named methodology or peer-reviewed citations are not independently verified at audit date from available training knowledge; the website and trade press references rely on practitioner testimonials and aggregate conversion/collection metrics framed as marketing claims rather than independently published studies. Named KOL relationships with verifiable digital presence are not independently verified at audit date, and no white papers with disclosed sample sizes have been confirmed, holding this score below the midpoint.
organisations.d3_rationale: Brand identity and messaging are broadly consistent across orthofi.com, LinkedIn, and ortho trade press placements, with a recognisable visual language and consistent positioning around financial accessibility and patient conversion. Some inconsistency exists between legacy product messaging (pre-any platform expansion) and current portfolio framing, and social channel posting cadence is irregular enough to create minor coherence gaps; no major acquisition integration issues are evident but subsidiary or partner co-branding is not clearly governed.
organisations.d4_rationale: OrthoFi — Orthodontic patient financing and engagement. Active in orthodontic trade press. G2/Capterra reviews generally positive. No major negative record. Growing market voice in ortho niche.
organisations.d5_rationale: Against its closest categorical competitors — Gaidge (ortho analytics and benchmarking), Dental Intelligence (ortho module within broader dental analytics), and OrthoChart/Ortho2 (practice management with financial components) — OrthoFi holds a recognisable brand position specifically in patient financing and case acceptance workflows, which is a narrower but defensible niche. Gaidge outperforms OrthoFi on benchmarking data credibility and outcomes transparency; Dental Intelligence has broader discoverability and stronger multi-channel content investment. OrthoFi's digital investment trajectory appears flat relative to Dental Intelligence's expanding content and integration marketing, placing it mid-pack in the competitive set rather than a clear leader.
organisations.scoring_notes: FDA clearance not applicable for SaaS financial platform — D2 scored solely on outcomes data, KOL presence, and published evidence. Named KOL relationships and peer-reviewed citations are not independently verified at audit date from training knowledge and scored conservatively per rubric. D4 review platform volume and response quality at account level are not independently verified at audit date and noted. Priority gap selected as D2 because a 12-month intervention publishing named outcome studies, practitioner-authored case data with disclosed methodology, and formalised KOL digital relationships would generate the greatest combined credibility lift and competitive differentiation, directly feeding MI improvement per framework weighting.

organisations.name: Archy
organisations.d1_rationale: tryarchy.com is indexed and functional with clear product messaging, and the brand surfaces on Google category searches for cloud dental practice management software. LinkedIn presence exists with regular posting activity, but trade press coverage in Dental Economics, Dentistry Today, and DSO Journal is sparse and largely limited to product announcement reposts rather than editorial features. Procurement platform and dental directory listing coverage is not independently verified at audit date from training knowledge; scored conservatively.
organisations.d2_rationale: No documentable FDA clearances, peer-reviewed studies, or named KOL relationships with verifiable digital presence have been identified for Archy as of audit date; the website relies on benefit-language marketing copy ('streamline your practice,' 'built for modern dentistry') without clinical outcomes data or methodology. VC backing provides some investor-facing credibility signal but does not satisfy the D2 sub-components. Scored 3.0 reflecting near-absence of documentable credibility evidence rather than a forced mid-range score.
organisations.d3_rationale: Brand identity and visual language appear consistent across the tryarchy.com website and LinkedIn, with coherent product-portfolio messaging for a single-platform PMS vendor. No significant acquired or subsidiary properties create integration risk. Minor gaps exist between website feature claims and LinkedIn content depth, but the coherence risk is low given the single-product focus; no material naming or contact accuracy issues detected.
organisations.d4_rationale: Archy — Dental practice management software. Intelligence suite launched Jun 2025. Limited independent review volume. Growing brand presence. No major negative record. Early-stage market voice for new entrant.
organisations.d5_rationale: Against named competitors Curve Dental, Oryx, and Dental Symphony, Archy occupies a weaker digital position: Curve Dental holds substantially greater trade press coverage, conference recognition, and D2 credibility signal from longer market tenure; Oryx has stronger KOL-linked content and clinical-workflow messaging; Dental Symphony targets a specific specialty niche with clearer differentiation messaging. Archy's modern UX positioning and VC growth trajectory are assets, but digital investment signal in content, SEO depth, and earned media lags all three named competitors at audit date.
organisations.scoring_notes: D1 procurement platform and dental directory listing coverage is not independently verified at audit date from training knowledge; scored conservatively at 5.5. D4 conference and award data is not independently verified at audit date; scored conservatively. D2 scored at 3.0 deliberately — marketing language on tryarchy.com does not satisfy rubric sub-components and no documentable clinical evidence, FDA clearance, or named KOL was identified. D2 selected as priority gap because a 12-month intervention (named KOL case studies, peer-reviewed use-case publication, G2 review velocity program) would generate the greatest combined OPS and MI improvement given current near-absence of credibility infrastructure versus all named competitors.

organisations.name: Zuub
organisations.d1_rationale: Zuub.com is indexed and functional with a defined product focus on dental RCM analytics, and the company maintains a LinkedIn presence with moderate activity. However, Google brand search returns limited third-party corroboration, trade press coverage is sparse, and the company does not appear prominently in procurement platform listings or major dental conference exhibitor directories as of audit date — discoverability is largely self-generated rather than ecosystem-amplified.
organisations.d2_rationale: No FDA clearance is applicable or claimed for a software analytics product, which is appropriate, but Zuub provides no documentable peer-reviewed outcomes studies, named KOL relationships with verifiable digital presence, or independently cited clinical/financial outcome data with methodology and sample sizes. Website content uses marketing-standard claims ('increase collections,' 'improve revenue') without supporting published evidence; no named white papers or third-party validation are verifiable, requiring a conservative score per rubric discipline.
organisations.d3_rationale: Brand identity across zuub.com, LinkedIn, and available social channels is broadly consistent in visual language and messaging around RCM/analytics positioning. However, the product portfolio page and social content show some inconsistency in how core features are framed, and there is limited evidence of coherent subsidiary or integration-partner co-branding. The worst-integrated case — partner/integration landing pages — shows incomplete or generic messaging that dilutes the core identity.
organisations.d4_rationale: Zuub — Dental RCM automation. Limited independent reviews at volume. Growing RCM segment coverage in dental trade press. No major negative record. Mid-stage market voice.
organisations.d5_rationale: Against its closest categorical competitors — Dental Intelligence (analytics-first, large review volume, strong trade press presence, DSO partnerships), Gaidge (practice analytics with long-standing brand recognition among independents), Adit (RCM-adjacent with broader practice management footprint), and Carestream Dental Analytics — Zuub trails on D1 discoverability, D2 credibility evidence, and D4 earned media. Zuub's RCM-specific positioning is a plausible differentiator but is not yet digitally operationalized at a level that closes the gap with Dental Intelligence in particular, which dominates organic category search and has named DSO reference accounts publicly documented.
organisations.scoring_notes: D2 is scored conservatively per rubric instruction: no documentable peer-reviewed studies, named KOLs, or regulatory filings were verifiable from available training data — not independently verified at audit date whether any private case studies or embargoed white papers exist. D4 trade press coverage is also not independently verified at audit date at granular article level but conservative scoring applied given absence of prominent indexed coverage. D5 competitive set confirmed as Dental Intelligence, Gaidge, Adit, and Carestream Dental Analytics. Priority gap assigned to D2 over D4 because a credibility-building intervention (named outcomes data, KOL co-authorship, published case studies) would generate compounding benefits across both OPS and MI — D4 improvement is partially downstream of D2 investment in this category.

organisations.name: Clearwave Dental
organisations.d1_rationale: clearwavedental.com appears indexed and carries product-focused pages, but LinkedIn posting cadence is inconsistent and the brand does not surface prominently in unprompted category searches (e.g., 'dental patient intake software') against entrenched competitors. Trade press coverage in Dental Economics and Dentistry Today is sparse relative to category leaders; conference exhibitor presence is not independently verified at audit date from training data at April 2026 resolution.
organisations.d2_rationale: Patient intake software in this category is not subject to FDA clearance requirements, so that sub-component is inapplicable. No named peer-reviewed studies, no published clinical outcomes data with methodology or sample size, and no named KOL relationships with verifiable digital presence could be confirmed. Website copy relies on outcome-claim marketing language ('reduce wait times,' 'improve patient experience') without documentable third-party evidence; this does not satisfy D2 sub-components under rubric rules.
organisations.d3_rationale: Brand naming appears consistent between domain, LinkedIn, and G2/Capterra listings under 'Clearwave Dental,' but messaging tone and depth vary across channels — LinkedIn posts are thin relative to website detail. No major acquisition integration issues are apparent, but the product portfolio boundary between Clearwave Dental and any parent or affiliated Clearwave entity (healthcare intake) creates a coherence risk that is not independently verified at audit date without confirmed corporate structure; scored conservatively on the worst-integrated observable case.
organisations.d4_rationale: Clearwave Dental — Patient intake and engagement platform. Capterra and G2 reviews generally positive. No major negative record. Growing market voice in intake/engagement niche.
organisations.d5_rationale: Against the named competitive set — NexHealth, Weave, and Doctible — Clearwave Dental occupies a materially weaker digital position on D1 (search surface and content depth), D2 (evidence base), and D4 (review volume and trade press). NexHealth carries stronger SEO authority, named DSO partnerships, and a larger G2 review corpus; Weave benefits from broader brand recognition and significant paid media investment; Doctible competes closely at similar tier with comparable digital maturity. Clearwave Dental's narrower dental-specific positioning provides some differentiation but does not yet translate into measurable digital share-of-voice advantage.
organisations.scoring_notes: D2 inapplicability of FDA clearance noted — this sub-component is formally N/A for SaaS patient intake tools; scored on remaining D2 sub-components only, which produces a low score given absence of documentable evidence. Corporate relationship between Clearwave Dental and broader Clearwave Inc. (general healthcare intake) is not independently verified at audit date and introduces D3 boundary risk. D4 review corpus size and Glassdoor data are not independently verified at audit date at April 2026 resolution; conservative scoring applied. Priority gap assigned to D2 because credibility signal deficiency directly suppresses both MI (no evidence base for procurement conversations) and OPS improvement potential, and a 12-month intervention (case study publication, KOL co-authorship, outcomes data release) is achievable within typical VC-backed product marketing cycles.

organisations.name: Dental Symphony
organisations.d1_rationale: dentalsymphony.com is indexable and functional but Google brand search returns limited organic depth beyond the homepage and a thin LinkedIn presence. Category search terms (e.g., 'cloud dental practice management software') yield no verifiable first-page placement against Curve Dental or Oryx. LinkedIn company page exists with low follower count and infrequent posting; dental trade press listings and procurement platform presence (e.g., Capterra, G2) appear sparse or not independently verified at audit date at scale.
organisations.d2_rationale: No named FDA clearance is applicable to a pure-software PMS, which is appropriate, but no published peer-reviewed outcomes studies, named KOL relationships with verifiable digital presence, or methodology-backed white papers are identifiable. Website copy uses marketing language ('intuitive,' 'seamless') without linking to documentable clinical or operational outcome data. Investor-facing credibility materials are not independently verified at audit date; founder-led context suggests limited institutional third-party validation signals.
organisations.d3_rationale: Core brand identity on dentalsymphony.com is consistent in naming and visual style, but cross-channel coherence is limited by low social posting cadence and a LinkedIn presence that does not consistently reinforce website messaging. No acquired or subsidiary properties to integrate, which reduces integration risk, but the product portfolio presentation across channels is shallow enough that coherence is present by default rather than by discipline. Worst-case channel (social/LinkedIn) drags the score.
organisations.d4_rationale: Dental Symphony — Practice management software. Limited independent reviews at volume. No major negative record. Thin market voice.
organisations.d5_rationale: Against direct categorical competitors Curve Dental, Oryx, and Archy, Dental Symphony trails materially on all D1–D4 sub-components: Curve Dental holds significantly stronger brand search volume, G2/Capterra review density, and trade press visibility; Oryx has an active content and social strategy with named KOL case studies; Archy demonstrates stronger LinkedIn engagement and targeted DSO-focused messaging. Dental Symphony's digital investment trajectory shows no verifiable acceleration signals (no recent funding announcements, product launch press, or conference presence) that would suggest narrowing of the gap within a 12-month horizon.
organisations.scoring_notes: D2 scored conservatively per rubric: no documentable peer-reviewed studies, named KOLs, or third-party clinical/operational outcome data identified; marketing copy on site does not satisfy sub-components. D4 G2/Capterra review volume is not independently verified at audit date precisely from training knowledge and scored accordingly. D1 SEO ranking position is not independently verified at audit date in real-time but inferred from low brand authority signals and absence of detectable trade press backlinks. Priority gap assigned to D2 over D4 because a credibility signal intervention (white papers, KOL partnerships, case studies with named outcomes) would simultaneously lift D4 (earned media) and D5 (competitive differentiation) with greater combined OPS and MI impact than a review-volume campaign alone.

organisations.name: iDentalSoft

Trade press indexation is sparse — no consistent presence in Dental Economics, Dentistry Today, or JADA search results — and conference/event discoverability is not independently verified at audit date from available training data. Procurement platform listing status (e.g., Capterra, G2, Software Advice) appears partial with thin review volume.
organisations.d2_rationale: No named FDA clearance is applicable to PMS software in the traditional device sense, but no peer-reviewed clinical outcome studies, named KOL relationships with verifiable digital presence, or published white papers with methodology and sample size could be identified for iDentalSoft. The website uses marketing-tier language ('easy to use,' 'all-in-one') without documentable third-party validation. Investor-facing credibility materials are not independently verified at audit date given VC-backed status with no public funding disclosures found in training data.
organisations.d3_rationale: Core brand identity on identalsoft.com and LinkedIn appears consistent in naming and visual treatment, but the depth of cross-channel coherence is limited by low overall channel presence — there are fewer surfaces to be inconsistent across, but also fewer to reinforce identity. No acquired or subsidiary properties were identified to test integration quality. Social media presence outside LinkedIn is thin, creating identity gaps rather than contradictions.
organisations.d4_rationale: iDentalSoft — Cloud-based practice management. Limited independent reviews. No major negative record. Thin market voice.
organisations.d5_rationale: Against direct competitors Curve Dental, Oryx, and Open Dental, iDentalSoft ranks materially lower on all four sub-components: Curve Dental holds significantly stronger trade press and review volume; Oryx demonstrates stronger LinkedIn activity and clinical content marketing; Open Dental benefits from a large open-source community generating organic discoverability and peer commentary. iDentalSoft does not demonstrably lead on any digital dimension within this competitive set, and digital investment trajectory signals are not independently verified at audit date, though VC backing suggests potential capacity not yet deployed.
organisations.scoring_notes: not independently verified at audit date: VC funding round details and investor-facing materials not publicly indexed. not independently verified at audit date: Conference participation and award recognition history not confirmable from training data. not independently verified at audit date: Exact G2/Capterra review counts as of April 2026 — scored conservatively. D2 scored at 3.0 due to complete absence of documentable clinical evidence, named KOLs, or peer-reviewed citations; marketing-tier language on site explicitly excluded per rubric. D2 selected as priority gap over D4 because a credibility signal intervention (white papers, named KOL engagement, outcome case studies) would simultaneously lift D4 (trade press pickup) and D5 positioning, generating the highest combined OPS and MI improvement per point gained.

organisations.name: Dental ClaimSupport
organisations.d1_rationale: Dental ClaimSupport maintains a functional website at dentalclaimsupport.com with blog content and service pages targeting DSOs and group practices, and holds a LinkedIn company presence with moderate follower count. However, trade press discoverability is thin — coverage in Dental Economics, Dentistry Today, or JADA is not consistently findable, and conference/event listing presence is limited relative to category peers. Procurement platform and G2/Capterra profile completeness is not independently verified at audit date from training knowledge and scored conservatively.
organisations.d2_rationale: No documentable FDA clearance is applicable to this claims management/RCM software category, which is not a regulated device. However, the D2 standard for DenTech still requires published clinical outcomes data with methodology, named KOL relationships with digital presence, and peer-reviewed citations — none of these are evidenced on the website or in discoverable third-party sources. Website content relies on benefit claims and testimonial-style language without named study authors, sample sizes, or measurable ROI data attributed to specific named clients with verifiable methodology. No named KOLs with independent digital presence have been identified.
organisations.d3_rationale: Brand and messaging are reasonably consistent across the website, LinkedIn, and visible social channels, with a coherent visual identity and consistent positioning around outsourced dental billing and claims management. However, cross-channel posting cadence on LinkedIn is irregular, and sub-brand or product portfolio coherence beyond the core service offering is limited and not clearly articulated across channels. No major acquisition integration issues are apparent, but the overall portfolio architecture is thin enough to limit score upside.
organisations.d4_rationale: Dental ClaimSupport — Dental billing service. Testimonial infrastructure on own site. Limited independent review volume but service-based model has different review dynamics. No major negative record.
organisations.d5_rationale: Against the named competitive set — Zentist and Vyne Dental — plus adjacent competitors Zuub and Apex EDI, Dental ClaimSupport occupies a mid-tier digital position. Zentist has stronger LinkedIn presence, more recent trade press coverage, and clearer outcome-oriented messaging with named DSO references. Vyne Dental benefits from a longer-established brand and broader product portfolio with higher search authority. Dental ClaimSupport's more services-first, outsourcing-led positioning differentiates it modestly but its digital investment signals lag the top two on content velocity, SEO depth, and analyst visibility.
organisations.scoring_notes: D2 is scored at 3.5 reflecting the absence of any documentable clinical outcomes data, named KOLs, or peer-reviewed citations — the segment is not FDA-regulated so that sub-component is inapplicable, but the remaining evidential standards are unmet and marketing language alone cannot compensate. Glassdoor/Indeed employer sentiment, G2/Capterra completeness, and conference appearance history are not independently verified at audit date from training knowledge and have been scored conservatively throughout. D2 is selected as priority gap because a 12-month intervention building named client case studies with quantified ROI, named KOL relationships, and earned trade press placements would generate the greatest combined OPS and MI improvement given the category is trust-sensitive and DSO procurement is committee-driven.

organisations.name: Patient News
organisations.d1_rationale: patientnews.com is indexable and functional with clear category positioning around dental direct mail and digital marketing; LinkedIn presence exists with moderate completeness but posting cadence is inconsistent. Trade press visibility is limited — no consistent presence in Dental Economics or Dentistry Today at a level suggesting active SEO or PR investment; conference discoverability is not independently verified at audit date from available training data.
organisations.d2_rationale: No documentable FDA clearance applies to this category (patient marketing software), which is appropriate, but the site relies on marketing language ('proven results,' 'grow your practice') without named peer-reviewed studies, specific outcome datasets with methodology, or publicly identified KOL relationships with verifiable digital presence. Case studies appear anecdotal rather than methodologically documented. No white papers or peer-reviewed citations identified; credibility signals are materially below standard for audit purposes.
organisations.d3_rationale: Brand identity between the website and LinkedIn appears reasonably consistent — name, visual language, and value proposition around dental direct mail plus digital marketing are cohesive. No major acquired or subsidiary properties identified that would introduce integration risk; however, the dual positioning (traditional direct mail legacy plus digital marketing) creates mild message tension across channels that is not fully resolved in messaging architecture.
organisations.d4_rationale: Patient News — Dental patient marketing and newsletter service. Long-established brand in dental marketing. Limited independent reviews. No major negative record. Established but not prominent market voice.
organisations.d5_rationale: Against direct competitors RevenueWell (stronger software platform integration and review management features with documented DSO traction), Weave (significantly larger digital footprint, G2/Capterra presence, named enterprise clients, higher review volume), and specialist dental digital ad agencies (locally variable but often stronger in paid search discoverability), Patient News holds a defensible niche in direct mail heritage but trails on digital-first discoverability, content authority, and third-party review platform presence. The competitive delta is negative on D1 and D4 versus RevenueWell and Weave specifically.
organisations.scoring_notes: D2 is structurally limited for a patient marketing software vendor since FDA clearance is inapplicable, but the absence of any documented clinical outcome data, named KOLs, or peer-reviewed citations is a genuine liability versus RevenueWell which has begun publishing ROI methodology data. D4 trade press recency is not independently verified at audit date with confidence for 2025-2026 and scored conservatively. D1 conference discoverability is not independently verified at audit date. Priority gap assigned to D2 because improving credibility signals (outcome data with sample sizes, named practice case studies with metrics, KOL partnerships) would simultaneously improve D4 and D5 trajectory — highest combined OPS/MI return per intervention point in this category.

organisations.name: Legwork (Patterson Companies)

LinkedIn presence exists but posting cadence is inconsistent and follower count is modest relative to category peers; dental trade press listings and procurement platform presence (e.g., Capterra, G2) are present but thin on reviews and recency.
organisations.d2_rationale: No documentable FDA clearance is applicable or evidenced for a patient communications SaaS product, which is appropriate to category, but no named peer-reviewed studies, clinical outcomes data with methodology, or named KOL relationships with verifiable digital presence could be confirmed — 'clinically proven' or equivalent marketing language appears on site without supporting citations. White papers are not independently verified at audit date as substantive clinical evidence; investor-facing credibility materials are absorbed into Patterson Companies' broader IR, not standalone. Score reflects near-absence of D2-qualifying evidence.
organisations.d3_rationale: Brand identity is split between 'Legwork' and 'Patterson Dental' ecosystem messaging across channels, with some web properties and social profiles referencing Patterson branding inconsistently. The worst-integrated case is social media, where Legwork's LinkedIn and Facebook pages show sporadic updates with messaging that does not consistently reflect current product positioning post-acquisition integration. Contact and support routing directs users to Patterson channels in some contexts, creating identity fragmentation.
organisations.d4_rationale: Legwork (Patterson Companies) — Patient engagement platform, Patterson subsidiary. Limited independent reviews under Legwork brand specifically. Patterson relationship provides distribution but not market voice. Mid-range.
organisations.d5_rationale: Against direct competitors Weave, NexHealth, and Solutionreach, Legwork trails materially on D1 (Weave and NexHealth both achieve stronger independent brand search presence and G2/Capterra review volumes), D2 (Weave and Solutionreach both publish more structured case study content), and D4 (Weave in particular has substantially higher trade press volume and clinician social commentary). Legwork's Patterson affiliation provides distribution credibility but suppresses standalone digital investment signals; NexHealth's VC-backed growth trajectory and Solutionreach's legacy review depth both outpace Legwork's current digital footprint.
organisations.scoring_notes: not independently verified at audit date: current posting frequency and follower trajectory on Legwork LinkedIn as of April 2026; Glassdoor sentiment for Legwork-specific headcount post-Patterson integration; whether any white papers published post-2024 contain methodology-grade clinical data. D3 scored on worst-integrated case (social/contact fragmentation) per rubric instruction. D2 scored conservatively given absence of documentable KOL, study, or regulatory evidence — marketing language on site does not qualify per rubric. Patterson acquisition integration is the dominant identity coherence risk across D1, D3, and D4.
